# Transcrição: 25. Trait 24 - Great Salespeople do not see themselves as selling, but serving and assisting.

- **Fonte:** 25. Trait 24 - Great Salespeople do not see themselves as selling, but serving and assisting.
- **Duração:** 01:11
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:11]

Great salespeople do not see themselves so much as selling as they do serving and helping out and assisting. The greats don't even like the idea that they're selling as much as this mission that they're on like I gotta serve I gotta help people I gotta go do this. It's like they're on this they got to do this because they're so sold they're taking care of people they actually feel hurt not because they lost a sale in some cases but because they feel like they let their customer down not helping them. Look they're more purpose driven than they are profit driven. They're driven by this idea that I have to get this product into their hands from this deep belief that the offer is more valuable than the money the offers more value than the risk maybe the customer takes the greats don't see themselves as selling so much as they see themselves as serving the customer the community and the betterment of everybody. Get sold on your proposition so deep the greatness the greatness just is a common ordinary everyday result of your belief in your product and service.
